---
name: Pattern request
about: Propose a new agentic UX pattern for the canonical library
title: "[pattern] <noun phrase, e.g. 'reversible-handoff'>"
labels: pattern-request
assignees: ''
---

## Pattern name

A short noun phrase. Lowercase, hyphenated. Example: `intent-handshake`.

## Problem

What user-facing failure does this pattern address? Reference the heuristic
(`aux.H01`–`H10`) or trust stage (`aux.T01`–`T04`) it supports.

## Pattern description

How does the pattern work? One paragraph. No marketing language.

## Diagram

Required. Even ASCII is fine. Patterns without diagrams are rejected per
CONTRIBUTING.md.

```
[ user ]  →  [ agent declares intent ]  →  [ user confirms or steers ]
```

## Worked example

Required. Code snippet, transcript, or runnable scenario.

## Anti-pattern

Required. What this pattern prevents — what the bad version looks like and
why it fails.

## References

Existing patterns this overlaps with, prior art, related research.
